geographical_info.location: Lakeland, Florida (Located in the high-tech corridor just off I-4 between Tampa and Orlando)
geographical_info.terrain: Built around a scenic system of Central Lakes, with campus infrastructure actively expanding along the east and west banks
geographical_info.campus_layout: Anchored by the iconic flagship IST building on the north end, residential and wellness facilities in the northeastern quadrant, student development in the southeast, and applied research in the northwest

building_names.IST: Innovation, Science, and Technology Building (Flagship building designed by Santiago Calatrava; houses 26 classrooms, 11 innovation labs including digital media, Makerspace, and supercomputing, plus fully digital library access)
building_names.ARC: Applied Research Center (90,000 sq. ft. facility housing research laboratories, senior design workshops, prototyping, and fabrication facilities)
building_names.SDC: Student Development Center (Equipped training facility with cardio/strength training equipment, fitness studios, and an outdoor 8-lane heated competition pool)
building_names.Wellness Center: Central campus hub (Contains primary campus dining facilities, the campus bookstore, Student Health Clinic, Ricoh Mail and Copy Center, and the Parking/Bursar offices)
building_names.Admissions Center: First stop for prospective students (Houses financial aid and admissions staff, and serves as the starting point for campus tours)
building_names.CCC: Campus Control Center (Home to the Florida Poly Police Department, facilities management, and administrative operations)
building_names.Residence Hall I: Phase I Housing (Operated by Capstone Management Partners, featuring modern suite-style living with 2, 3, and 4-bedroom options)
building_names.Residence Hall II: Phase II Housing (Traditional dorm living with a community feel, equipped with game rooms and common study areas on all five floors)
building_names.Phase III: Next-Generation Student Living (Newest residential expansion offering modern student apartments)
building_names.SunTrax: Associated Research Facility (Located off-campus in Auburndale; a 400-acre site with a 2.25-mile oval track for testing connected and autonomous vehicles)

food_places.Phoenix Food Fusion 'The Fuse': Located in the Wellness Center. An all-you-care-to-eat buffet featuring live stations (grill, pizza, vegan, salad) and eco-friendly grab-and-go options.
food_places.Einstein Bros. Bagels: Located in the Wellness Center. A daily go-to hangout for fresh bagels, sandwiches, and coffee.
food_places.Fire + Ash: Located in the Wellness Center. A student-favorite rotating concept that reinvents itself every 4-6 weeks (serving Mediterranean, burgers, pho, etc.) based on student text-line feedback.
food_places.Mosaic Café: Located in the IST Building. Offers grab-and-go snacks, sandwiches, parfaits, and Starbucks beverages between classes.
food_places.Starship Delivery Robots: High-tech campus-wide food delivery. Autonomous robots that students can order from via an app to have meals delivered anywhere on campus sidewalks.

activities_and_hangouts.The Nest: Student lounge located inside the Wellness Center dedicated to relaxing, socializing, and gaming.
activities_and_hangouts.SDC Pool: Outdoor 8-lane heated competition pool at the Student Development Center, open for leisure swimming and physical technology testing.
activities_and_hangouts.Intramural Sports: Organized recreation events and sports taking place in the green spaces around the SDC and central campus.
activities_and_hangouts.RAD Makerspace: Rapid Application Development lab in the IST where engineering and tech students hang out to build, prototype, and tinker.
activities_and_hangouts.Campus Oak Grove & Pergolas: Scenic outdoor architectural walkways surrounding the IST building, popular for studying, photography, and enjoying the Florida weather.

parking_lots.Lots 3, 4, 5, 6, 8, and 10: Designated for Students with Resident/Commuter Permits
parking_lots.Lots 8 and 10: Designated for Students with Economy Permits (a budget-friendly parking option)
parking_lots.Visitor Parking Loop: Located at the Admissions Center for prospective students and special event guests
parking_lots.Lot 1 & 2 Areas: Generally reserved for Faculty, Staff, and accessible/disability parking near main buildings like the IST and Wellness Center

emergency_buttons: Blue Light Emergency Phones situated along Polytechnic Circle (the main campus loop road)
Blue Light Emergency Phones located throughout major student parking areas (Lots 3, 4, 8, and 10)
Emergency call boxes stationed near pathways connecting Residence Halls I and II to the main academic buildings
Direct walk-up emergency assistance at the Campus Control Center (Police Department)

specific_resources.digital_library: IST Building (Accessed virtually; collection includes databases, reference materials, journals, and ebooks)
specific_resources.mail_services: Wellness Center (Ricoh Mail and Copy Center)
